title: Sustainability: version policy, staleness cadence, upstream-contribution + reviewer rotation plan
context: After a successful adoption, the standards must be maintained: terminologies evolve (FHIR/mCODE versions, ontology releases), regulatory text is updated, and expert reviewers must rotate to avoid stagnation. Without a version policy, staleness cadence, and reviewer rotation plan, the standards will become outdated and potentially unsafe. Outcomes should be tracked as consent-first registries adopted, not downloads. Depends on rare-cancer-registry-templates-adopt-001.
objective: Document the sustainability plan (`governance/sustainability-plan.md`) covering: (1) semantic versioning policy for the CDE set, crosswalks, consent templates, and governance docs; (2) staleness cadence — per-source-type `validUntil` review intervals (ontologies on major release, regulatory mapping on short cadence) and the auto-flag/withhold process for stale HIGH-tier claims; (3) upstream-contribution path (OHDSI oncology workgroup, GA4GH driver project, ERN/rare-disease network); and (4) reviewer rotation plan for all roles (maintainer, licence reviewer, standards reviewer, credentialed HIGH-tier reviewers).
acceptanceCriteria: Version policy defines semantic versioning for the CDE set, crosswalks, consent templates, and governance docs; major/minor/patch semantics described.
Staleness cadence specifies review intervals per source type (ontology major release, FHIR IG version, regulatory text on short cadence) and the auto-flag/withhold process per the prov-001 model.
Upstream-contribution path identifies at least one standards body (OHDSI, GA4GH, or ERN-affiliated network) and describes the contribution process.
Reviewer rotation plan specifies how each role (maintainer, licence, standards, oncologist+advocate, bioethics/legal) is recruited, rotated, and transitioned.
Outcomes tracking method defined: consent-first registries adopted, conformance reports issued, upstream contributions — not downloads or stars.
output: A `governance/sustainability-plan.md` covering version policy, staleness cadence, upstream-contribution path, reviewer rotation, and outcomes tracking, committed via PR with maintainer sign-off.
